events.name: The Execution of Gol D. Roger
events.outcome: The Pirate King dies at Loguetown — and his last words about the One Piece ignite the Great Pirate Era. In-world this is decades before the story; the reader watches it in the chapter 1 prologue, so it is charted from chapter 1.
events.source_ref: Hand-authored. Chapter 1 prologue: Roger's execution at Loguetown and the words that launch the era. occurred_chapter follows the reader-time rule. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.

events.name: The Straw Hat Promise
events.outcome: Shanks leaves his straw hat with Luffy — to be returned when Luffy becomes a great pirate. The whole voyage is this promise being kept.
events.source_ref: Hand-authored. Chapter 1 (Romance Dawn): Shanks gives Luffy the straw hat as he departs Foosha Village. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.

events.name: The Fall of Axe-Hand Morgan
events.outcome: Luffy and Zoro topple the tyrant of the 153rd Marine Branch — the crew's first fight as a crew.
events.source_ref: Hand-authored. Shells Town: Morgan is beaten and Zoro sails with Luffy (~ch. 6). The exact defeat chapter is a reading of the arc. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.

events.name: Buggy Blasted Away at Orange Town
events.outcome: Nami ties Buggy's scattered parts together and Luffy sends the rest flying — Orange Town is free, and the map's longest-running grudge begins.
events.source_ref: Hand-authored. Orange Town: Luffy defeats Buggy with Nami's help (~ch. 20). The exact chapter is a reading of the arc. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.

events.name: Kuro of a Hundred Plans Falls
events.outcome: The butler's three-year plan dies on the beach; Syrup Village never learns it was ever in danger, because Usopp the liar tells them nothing happened.
events.source_ref: Hand-authored. Syrup Village: Luffy defeats Captain Kuro (~ch. 39). The exact chapter is a reading of the arc. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.

events.name: Zoro vs. Mihawk — the Duel at the Baratie
events.outcome: The world's greatest swordsman opens Zoro's chest and spares his life for his ambition. Zoro's vow to Luffy — never to lose again until he takes the title — is the oath the whole swordsman's arc hangs on.
events.source_ref: Hand-authored. The Baratie, open sea (~ch. 50–51): Mihawk defeats Zoro; the never-again vow follows. Explicit lng/lat: the Baratie is a floating restaurant with no island row; position matches the voyage's Baratie anchor. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.

events.name: Don Krieg Defeated at the Baratie
events.outcome: The self-styled strongest armada of East Blue breaks against one rubber pirate defending a floating restaurant; Sanji sails soon after.
events.source_ref: Hand-authored. The Baratie: Luffy defeats Don Krieg (~ch. 66). Explicit lng/lat: the Baratie has no island row; position matches the voyage's Baratie anchor. The exact chapter is a reading of the arc. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.

events.name: The Fall of Arlong Park
events.outcome: Luffy brings Arlong Park down on its owner's head. Eight years of Cocoyasi's tribute end, and Nami joins the crew for real — the hat on her head while she cried is the hinge of East Blue.
events.source_ref: Hand-authored. Conomi Islands: Luffy defeats Arlong and the Park collapses (~ch. 93). The exact chapter is a reading of the arc. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.

events.name: The Escape from the Execution Platform
events.outcome: On the platform where Roger died, Buggy's blade falls — and lightning falls first. Luffy grins where the Pirate King grinned, and Smoker decides this one is his to chase.
events.source_ref: Hand-authored. Loguetown: the execution attempt and the lightning strike (~ch. 99). The exact chapter is a reading of the arc. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.

events.name: Into the Grand Line
events.outcome: The barrel oath in the storm, and the Going Merry rides Reverse Mountain up and over — East Blue ends, the Grand Line begins.
events.source_ref: Hand-authored. Reverse Mountain (~ch. 100–101): the crew's declarations over the barrel and the ascent into the Grand Line. Charted at ch. 101 with the island's debut. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.

locations.name: Kingdom of the Cherry Trees / Kingdom of Drum
Milky Road / La Route de Nuages

swords.name: Yoru
swords.description: Kokuto Yoru is a long black saber, over 2 metres long, shaped like a crucifix, with a hilt adorned with several precious stones, but no scabbard. Its shape resembles a two-handed version of the medieval Großes Messer. Mihawk puts it on his back when not in use, securing it with straps.

swords.name: Murakumogiri
swords.description: It's a sharp weapon, being slightly taller than he is, standing at 6m66. His spear is striped red and yellow (in the anime, the stripes are brown) and ends in a spherical pommel. At the end of the spear is a large, curved blade attached to the spear by a solid piece with a snake motif.

swords.name: Ace
swords.description: Ace is a full-sized sword, with a slightly curved blade featuring an ornate saw pattern on both sides. The sword has a yellow/orange hilt, with a diamond-shaped pattern, a golden guard, and a thin hand guard, covering from the top of the guard to the sheath, which is black.

swords.name: Shodai Kitetsu
swords.description: She's unknown, but her appearance must be quite similar to that of Sandai Kitetsu.

swords.name: Wadô Ichimonji
swords.description: The sword has a pure white hilt with a circular guard. It was described as "magnificent" by Tashigi when she noticed it in Loguetown.

swords.name: Nidai Kitetsu
swords.description: The Nidai Kitetsu has an atypical appearance. Its hilt is a kind of cross, with the ends wider than the center, and the blade has a unique wavelet pattern. The sword is very similar to the Sandai Kitetsu. Its hilt and scabbard were originally blue, but the color was later changed to purple.

swords.name: Shusui
swords.description: Shusui is a black blade featuring a hamon with white wave patterns (reddish-purple in the anime). Its black scabbard is decorated with several red circles, themselves divided into three thirds. Its hilt takes the form of an eight-petal golden flower, with a hole on each side of the blade.

swords.name: Enma
swords.description: Enma is a katana with a cloverleaf-shaped guard, identical to Ame no Habakiri except for the color. Its scabbard is entirely mauve and decorated with several golden flowers.

swords.name: Ame no Habakiri
swords.description: Ame no Habakiri is a white katana with a cloverleaf guard, identical to Enma except for the color.

swords.name: Shigure
swords.description: The Shigure is a katana with a green hilt and a guard composed of four petals, or square sides. The blade itself is normal. The sheath is elaborate, the upper half being green and bumpy; the lower half is white and smooth. There are integrated circular patterns all over the sheath.

swords.name: Sandai Kitetsu
swords.description: The Sandai Kitetsu has an atypical appearance. Its hilt is a kind of cross, with the ends wider than the center, and the blade has a unique wavelet pattern.

swords.name: Yubashiri
swords.description: Yubashiri is black with a yellow dot on the handle. The same applies to the scabbard. The blade is standard.

swords.name: Oto
swords.description: Oto and Kogarashi are katanas with a particular shape: they have tsuba (guard) in the shape of a cross (as for the Yubashiri), their wrists are red, but they have stabbing blades, straight and tapered at the tip, not curved like most of the swords in One Piece. Although we don't know what special properties they have, they seem to be quite famous.

swords.name: kogarashi
swords.description: Oto and Kogarashi are katanas with a particular shape: they have tsuba (guard) in the shape of a cross (as for the Yubashiri), their wrists are red, but they have stabbing blades, straight and tapered at the tip, not curved like most of the swords in One Piece. Although we don't know what special properties they have, they seem to be quite famous.

swords.name: Raiu
swords.description: The Raiu is a standard-looking Nodachi. Its hilt is square and yellow. Its handle is red. Its blade has a classic appearance.

swords.name: Durandal
swords.description: Durandal is a kind of court sword in length that could be compared to a rapier; it is almost equal to Cavendish's height when seated. Its sheath and hilt are colored vertically, with a black and white contrast. The handle is also adorned with several rings and carved flowers.

swords.name: Shirauo
swords.description: Shirauo is an extremely long nodachi, being about as tall as Almond itself and taller than average-sized people. The sword has a fish-shaped guard, and a blue handle with fish-scale motifs.

swords.name: Pretzel
swords.description: It's a long sword with a thin black handle. Cracker says it's unique, but he has several when he fights Luffy.

swords.name: Konpira
swords.description: Konpira is a Meito, which suggests that the blade is of very high quality.

luffy_gears.name: Gear 2 / Gear Second
luffy_gears.description: It's a technique that improves power and speed in combat by increasing blood pressure, by positioning the body as shown in the image, which is normally impossible for a normal human, as the body can't withstand that much blood pressure. But since Luffy's body is made of rubber, he can handle the pressure.

luffy_gears.name: Gear 3 / Gear Third
luffy_gears.description: This technique greatly enhances Luffy's fighting power. To activate it, he bites down on his thumb and blows into it. The exhaled air spreads throughout his arm, allowing him to inflate his arm like a balloon, making it as big as a giant's arm. He can also use it with other parts of his body (notably his stomach or his foot). Once again, only Luffy can use this technique.

luffy_gears.name: Gear 4 / Gear Fourth
luffy_gears.description: A recent technique unlocked by his training, it allows him to inflate, improve his offensive fluid (Weapon's Haki) and retract his arms, then release them. He thus improves his power even more than with the Gear 3. He normally triggers it by biting his wrist.

luffy_gears.name: Gear 4 / Gear Fourth : Bound Man
luffy_gears.description: The first time he used it was against the great privateer Doflamingo. And the second time he used it, he managed to activate it by sheer force of will, whereas he usually bites his wrist, and while controlled by Doflamingo's Parasite.

luffy_gears.name: Gear 4 / Gear Fourth : Tank Man
luffy_gears.description: He uses it for the first time against Cracker, a technique very similar to Gomu Gomu no Fuusen.

luffy_gears.name: Gear 4 / Gear Fourth : Snake Man
luffy_gears.description: It's not as imposing as the Boundman. However, it is much faster. First used against Katakuri in Mirror World

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Pistol
luffy_techniques.description: His standard attack, Luffy hits his opponent with an elastic punch at both long and short range.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Rocket
luffy_techniques.description: He holds on to an object, stretches his arms out as far as possible, then hurls himself at enemies at full speed. Very useful for covering long distances quickly.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Muchi
luffy_techniques.description: He kicks with his leg in a circular motion, stretching it out to sweep away dozens of enemies at once.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Tonkachi
luffy_techniques.description: He wraps his limbs around his enemy, throwing him to the ground while uncoiling his limbs to spin him around.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Fuusen
luffy_techniques.description: A defensive technique, it inflates its body like a balloon, enabling it to deflect certain projectiles like cannonballs.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Kama
luffy_techniques.description: He stretches out his arms to the sides and lunges at his enemies, mowing down dozens in the process.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Gatling Gun
luffy_techniques.description: He throws a flurry of punches at his enemy at impressive speed, giving the impression that he has several arms.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Bulet
luffy_techniques.description: He stretches his arm far back, then brings it forward to deliver a short-range punch.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Yari
luffy_techniques.description: He joins his toes and stretches his legs like a spear to strike his enemy.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Kane
luffy_techniques.description: He holds on to his foe, stretches his neck backwards and then throws it forward to deliver a skull blow to his opponent.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Bazooka
luffy_techniques.description: He first stretches both arms backwards as far as possible before striking his opponent with as much force as possible using the palms of both hands, which generally throws the opponent far away.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Ono
luffy_techniques.description: He stretches his leg vertically and brings it down to crush his target. This attack is so powerful that even ships and buildings can shatter on impact.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Kazagurama
luffy_techniques.description: Firmly anchored on his legs, he rolls up on himself, grabs a large object or an enemy and unrolls at full speed to hit all opponents around him (without differentiating between friend and foe).

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Tate
luffy_techniques.description: He places the palm of his hand towards his opponent, stretches his fingers and blocks the enemy's attack.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Ami
luffy_techniques.description: He stretches out his arms and fingers to form a giant net in which he traps his opponent, or cannonballs. Then he spins around, bringing his arms and fingers back to him, causing whatever he's trapped in his net to fall to the ground.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Ikebana
luffy_techniques.description: He tore off his ship's mast and planted it in Laboon's still fresh wound.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Stamp
luffy_techniques.description: He throws his foot straight at his enemy, striking him in the face with the sole of his foot.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Bowgun
luffy_techniques.description: He holds on to his opponent, wrapping himself upwards and placing his feet level with his enemy's waist. He unrolls himself, throwing his opponent into the air.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Dameda
luffy_techniques.description: He uses this technique to prevent someone from taking what belongs to him.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Baku Baku
luffy_techniques.description: He swells his mouth and bites the enemy.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Marunokogiri
luffy_techniques.description: He stretches out his arms, grabs his opponent and propels himself towards him, spinning like a circular saw.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Shotgun
luffy_techniques.description: He first performs a Gomu Gomu no Pistol, then "vibrates" his arm, giving the illusion that he's hitting his opponent several times at once.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Storm
luffy_techniques.description: He inflates himself with the Gomu Gomu no Fuusen and winds himself wide, then expels all his air to the ground as he uncoils. This enables him to take to the air and deliver a flurry of punches as he whirls around.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Hanabi
luffy_techniques.description: He delivers a flurry of punches and kicks to his enemy, giving the impression of seeing dozens of fists and feet.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Bohhh
luffy_techniques.description: He empties his mind and unconsciously dodges all his opponent's attacks, but can't counter-attack as he thinks of nothing.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Tako
luffy_techniques.description: Same as the Gomu Gomu no Bohhh except that this time all its limbs are soft, but this allows it to perform an attack, the Gomu Gomu no Tako Hanabi.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Tako Hanabi
luffy_techniques.description: He hits the scenery in front of him, and his opponent catches the ricochets of his previous shots.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Rifle
luffy_techniques.description: He stretches his arm back and wraps it around himself to deliver a devastating punch as he uncoils it.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Golden Rifle
luffy_techniques.description: He stretches his arm, trapped in a golden sphere, backwards, and wraps it around himself to deliver a devastating punch as he uncoils it.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Hook
luffy_techniques.description: He delivers a powerful hook to his enemy.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Flail
luffy_techniques.description: He spins one of his arms above him, then brings it down on his enemy.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Cannon
luffy_techniques.description: He warms up with a flurry of punches in the air, then joins both arms to throw them forward at the enemy.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Volcano
luffy_techniques.description: He throws his leg into the air, finally destroying the building he's standing in like a volcanic eruption.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Ame
luffy_techniques.description: He leaps over his enemies to land a flurry of punches.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Stamp Gatling
luffy_techniques.description: He throws a flurry of kicks at his enemy at impressive speed, giving the impression that he has several feet. This technique was used against Aokiji, Kizaru and Akainu at the same time during the Battle of Marineford.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Snake Shot
luffy_techniques.description: He half-opens his hand, phalanges forward, as if reaching for something. He then stretches out his arm, undulating and charging at the enemy like a snake attacking its target, finally hitting it with his fingertips.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Sei Ken Teki
luffy_techniques.description: Luffy delivers a powerful punch, at the same time as the Kung-Fu Dugong he's fighting.

luffy_techniques.name: Mizu Mizu no Pistol
luffy_techniques.description: Luffy spits water in his "Aqua Luffy" form.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Hanabi Ougon Botan
luffy_techniques.description: He delivers a flurry of punches, trapping the latter in a golden sphere. This technique is used to destroy the lightning cloud created by Ener.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Ougon RIfle
luffy_techniques.description: He coils his arm and then delivers a punch, which is trapped in a golden sphere, to his opponent as he uncoils it. This technique is used to defeat Ener.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Champion Rifle
luffy_techniques.description: He performs a Gomu Gomu no Rifle with his arm equipped with Mr 3's wax armor. This technique is used against Magellan.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Champion Gatling Gun
luffy_techniques.description: He performs a Gomu Gomu no Gatling Gun with his arm equipped with Mr 3's wax armor. This technique is used against Magellan.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Champion Bazooka
luffy_techniques.description: He performs a Gomu Gomu no Bazooka with his arm equipped with Mr 3's wax armor. This technique is used against Magellan.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Champion Stamp
luffy_techniques.description: He performs a Gomu Gomu no Stamp with his arm equipped with Mr 3's wax armor. This technique is used against Magellan.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Jet Migawari
luffy_techniques.description: He uses Baggy as protection against attacks involving bladed weapons. This technique is used against Mihawk.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Pachinko
luffy_techniques.description: Luffy plants his feet in the ground, tucks his head between his shoulders and uses his arms to project an object like a slingshot. This technique is used against Foxy's crew.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Jet Pistol
luffy_techniques.description: faster and more powerful.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Jet Rifle
luffy_techniques.description: faster and more powerful.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Jet Stamp
luffy_techniques.description: faster and more powerful.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Jet Missile
luffy_techniques.description: faster and more powerful.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Jet Bazooka
luffy_techniques.description: a thousand times faster and more powerful. This attack gives the impression that Luffy is leaving both arms behind as he runs towards his opponent just after throwing his arms back.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Jet Axe
luffy_techniques.description: much faster and more powerful, causing greater damage.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Jet Yari
luffy_techniques.description: faster and more powerful.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Jet Bullet
luffy_techniques.description: faster and more powerful.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Jet Rocket
luffy_techniques.description: Luffy delivers a super-powerful and extremely fast back-kick

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Jet Mace
luffy_techniques.description: (with both arms at the same time) lightning-fast. This attack is effective enough to leave the mark of both fists on the opponent.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Jet Hammer
luffy_techniques.description: He sends his opponent to the ground by grabbing him by the feet.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Jet Gatling Gun
luffy_techniques.description: Luffy's ultimate attack in Gear 2: a burst of Chewing Jet Pistol that often leaves the opponent no time to counterattack.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu No Jet Tonkachi
luffy_techniques.description: faster and more powerful.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Gigant Pistol
luffy_techniques.translation: Chewing Punch Géant / Gum-Gum Gigant pistol
luffy_techniques.description: The attack is fairly slow, but its destructive effect is gigantic.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Gigant Ono
luffy_techniques.description: This is one of Gear 3's most devastating attacks, allowing Luffy to severely damage a ship containing 500 Navy soldiers.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Gigant Bazooka
luffy_techniques.description: Luffy swells his two fists to attack: it's with this technique that he defeats Oars, the most powerful zombie of the great corsair Gecko Moria.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Gigant Rifle
luffy_techniques.description: Luffy gains height and executes a giant Chewing Spin to finish off his opponent.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Gigant Fuusen
luffy_techniques.description: A much bigger Chewing Balloon.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Giant Thor Axe
luffy_techniques.description: Luffy inflates his leg and foot and lifts them high into the electricity-filled clouds, then lowers them with force and speed onto his opponent. Technique used to defeat Shiki the Golden Lion in the film Strong World.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Snake Shot
luffy_techniques.description: Luffy throws his arm at his opponent, making him undulate like a snake, hence the name, and strikes him with his fingers. First used against Hody Jones.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Thank You Fire
luffy_techniques.description: This technique precisely returns the projectile to the sender with twice the power. Luffy forms a square with the thumb and forefinger of both hands to create a sight and aim at the target. This technique is used against the Yeti Cool Brothers.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Soucoupe Volante
luffy_techniques.description: Luffy twirls his legs to attack his enemies and seems able to fly. This technique amuses Usopp.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Tako Stamp
luffy_techniques.description: Luffy uses Weapon's Haki on both legs and sends a flurry of kicks similar to "Gomu Gomu no stamp gatling".

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Eagle Bazooka
luffy_techniques.description: Luffy does his Gomu Gomu no Jet Bazooka technique again, but with the Offensive Fluid on both arms to create a more powerful attack.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Spring Bullet
luffy_techniques.description: Luffy picks up speed by bouncing with the offensive fluid, then lunges towards his opponent with a reinforced punch.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Tsuppari
luffy_techniques.description: Sumo technique that consists in using the palms of the hands to knock the opponent backwards.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Red Hawk
luffy_techniques.description: After switching his arm to Gear 2, Luffy strengthens it with the Offensive Fluid and strikes his opponent with full force, even creating flames.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Giant Jet Lance
luffy_techniques.description: A giant Gear 2 move thanks to Lily the giant with feet.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Jet Mace
luffy_techniques.description: In Gear 2, Luffy strikes with a back kick.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Jet Gatling
luffy_techniques.description: in Gear 2, Luffy uses Weapon Fluid with a super-powered Jet Rafale.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Jet Spear
luffy_techniques.description: In Gear 2, Luffy uses a much faster Chewing Lance. A technique powerful enough to pierce metal in a single blow.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Hawk Gatling
luffy_techniques.description: Gomu Gomu no Gatling with gear 2 and weapon fluid.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Hawk Rifle
luffy_techniques.description: Improved version of Gomu Gomu no Rifle combined with Weapon's Haki and Gear 2.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Eléphant Gun
luffy_techniques.description: Luffy switches his arm to Gear 3, strengthens it with Offensive Fluid and delivers a gigantic punch to his opponent.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Eléphant Gatling
luffy_techniques.description: Luffy switches both arms to Gear 3, reinforces it with Weapon's Haki and delivers a series of gigantic punches to his opponent.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Grizzly Magnum
luffy_techniques.description: Luffy stretches his arms back and strikes his opponent

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Thor Elephant Gun
luffy_techniques.description: Luffy uses the Elephant Gun when he's in the air, striking his opponent and literally stunning him.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Gigant Tsuppari
luffy_techniques.description: Similar to Gomu Gomu no Tsuppari but much more powerful

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Grizzly Corkscrew Magnum
luffy_techniques.description: Similar to the Gomu Gomu no Grizzly Magnum, Luffy combines two Gigant Riffles reinforced by Haki de l'armement.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Red Roc
luffy_techniques.description: After switching his arm to Gear 3, Luffy hits his opponent with full force, even creating flames.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Roc Gun
luffy_techniques.description: After switching his arm to Gear 3, Luffy uses his supreme weapon's haki to hit his opponent at full speed, much faster than the "Elephant Gun".

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Roc Gatling
luffy_techniques.description: After switching his arm to Gear 3, Luffy uses his supreme weapons haki and delivers a series of gigantic punches to his opponent.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Kong Gun
luffy_techniques.description: Luffy retracts his fists into his arm like a spring before letting go.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Rhino Schneider
luffy_techniques.description: Luffy retracts his feet into his legs like a spring before letting go.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Culverin
luffy_techniques.description: Luffy sends out his fist, changing its trajectory once launched to hit its target.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Leo Bazooka
luffy_techniques.description: Luffy uses a technique similar to the Jet Bazooka, but he uses his hands as if he were going to use a "Kaméhaméha".

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no King Kong Gun
luffy_techniques.description: Luffy, while in Gear Fourth, uses Gear Third to inflate his arm, making him much more powerful and massive than before.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Kong Organ
luffy_techniques.description: This attack is similar to the Gomu Gomu no Kong Gun, but Luffy multiplies his arms, and applies it like a Gomu Gomu no Jet Gatling Gun.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Kong Gatling
luffy_techniques.description: This attack is a Gomu Gomu no kong Gun, but is used in the same way as a Gomu Gomu no Jet Gatling Gun. This technique is made possible by Ryuo's recent mastery.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Cannon Ball
luffy_techniques.description: The opponent's attack is absorbed and blocked by Luffy's stomach, then after a while, Luffy powerfully returns the attack like cannon fire.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Jet Culverin
luffy_techniques.description: Faster version of the Gomu Gomu no Culverin in Boundman. Luffy compresses his fist, then releases it. He uses Python to make his fist follow his opponent's movements. The more his arm stretches, the faster he goes.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Black Mamba
luffy_techniques.description: This technique is similar to the Gomu Gomu no Kong Organ in Boundman, but coupled with the Culverin, Luffy arches both arms back, compresses them and then releases them. Since there are several trajectories, the Black Mamba is more complicated to avoid.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no King Kobra
luffy_techniques.description: Larger version of Gomu Gomu no Jet Culverin. This attack is released after Luffy has compressed his fist into his forearm. Its trajectory circles Luffy to increase speed and power.

luffy_techniques.name: Robot Allié
luffy_techniques.description: Luffy wraps his arms and legs around his opponent to control him.

luffy_techniques.name: Robot Allié Punch
luffy_techniques.description: Luffy uses his opponent to throw a punch.

luffy_techniques.name: Robot Allié Kick
luffy_techniques.description: Luffy uses his opponent to kick other enemies.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Hook
luffy_techniques.description: Luffy gives the enemy a powerful hook

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Flail
luffy_techniques.description: Luffy spins his arm in the air and brings it down on his opponent like a sledgehammer.

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Dawn Rocket
luffy_techniques.description: Gomu Gomu no Dawn Rocket

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Dawn Gatling
luffy_techniques.description: Gomu Gomu no Dawn Gatling

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Dawn Whip
luffy_techniques.description: Gomu Gomu no Dawn Whip

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Lightning
luffy_techniques.description: Gomu Gomu no Lightning

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Giant
luffy_techniques.description: Gomu Gomu no Giant

luffy_techniques.name: Gomu Gomu no Bajrang Gun
luffy_techniques.description: Gomu Gomu no Bajrang Gun
